_human_review.notes: SA Stage B generated/autolabeled labels were reconciled by Codex for internal benchmark cleanup; project-owner approval is still required before promotion-ready status.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: adequacy decision
standard contractual clauses
DSAR

Domestic transfers: cybersecurity telemetry and audit logs may be relayed to the Sand Data & AI Authority (SDAA) supervised government cloud through “NadrahNet” connectors; such transfers remain within the Kingdom and follow PDPL transfer controls.

Cross-border transfers occur only with an adequacy decision or a permit and are documented in our transfer registry; standard contractual clauses with processors include onward transfer restrictions and breach notification duties.

Security: we apply pseudonymisation, encryption at rest and in transit, privileged access management, and quarterly access reviews aligned to national cybersecurity control baselines (v1.0, internal standard).

Complaints: you may contact our DPO office at dpo@rimal-oasis.example.sa; unresolved PDPL complaints can be raised through the Kingdom data authority’s public portal in accordance with applicable guidance.
MNPI
ir@rimal-oasis.example.sa
privacy@rimal-oasis.example.sa
dpo@rimal-oasis.example.sa
issuer blackout windows specified by the national exchange
must not be selectively disclosed to analysts or investors
Cross-border transfers occur only with an adequacy decision or a permit
from quarter-end until our results are publicly posted on the exchange portal
“Iqama No.: XX-0000-XX”
we do not require social media handles or unrelated background data
we avoid storing full document images unless legally mandated
we minimise retention of ID scans; where verification is required, only redacted copies are retained
unannounced board approvals
